subject: Antoinette — thank you for being one of our most loyal customers
body_plain:
Hi Antoinette,

I'm Anthony, the founder of Uncle May's Produce. I was going through our customer data this week and realized you've ordered from us 3 times — that puts you in a tiny group of customers who've actually given us a second and third try out to Auburn Gresham. I wanted to reach out personally and say thank you. We're a small operation a few weeks into the Chicago delivery side of the business, and customers like you are the reason this is going to work.

Two things I'd love your help with:

1. 15 minutes on the phone — I'm trying to understand what made you keep coming back, what almost stopped you the second time, and what you'd want us to do next. Your perspective is more valuable than any survey: https://calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview (https://calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview). Pick any time that works.

2. A standing offer. As a thank you, the next box you order is on me up to $50 — just reply to this email and tell me which Wednesday and I'll comp it. No promo code games, no minimum cart shenanigans. You earned it.

If now isn't the right time, no pressure — but I'd really value hearing from you whenever you have a few minutes. Reply, call/text (312) 972-2595, or grab a slot on my calendar.

Thank you for trusting us with your grocery order.

Anthony Ivy
Founder, Uncle May's Produce
(312) 972-2595
anthony@unclemays.com

subject: Hey Alisia — was there an issue with your Uncle May's order?
body_plain:
Hey Alisia,

I'm Anthony, the founder of Uncle May's Produce. I noticed you tried to place an order with us twice last week — once on Wednesday and again Thursday afternoon — and both times something didn't go through at the payment step. I wanted to reach out personally and figure out what happened.

You were ordering the family box with pinto beans, delivery to the Clyde Ave address in Calumet City. The promo code FRESH10 was applied both times — that part was working. So I'm guessing it was a card issue, a browser issue, or maybe you just got pulled away.

If you still want the box, three ways forward — pick whichever is easiest:

1. Try checkout again at unclemays.com/shop (https://unclemays.com/shop), code FRESH10 for $10 off. We pushed several fixes to the payment form this week so it should behave better than last time.
2. Take your order over the phone in 5 minutes — call/text me at (312) 972-2595.
3. Grab 10 minutes on my calendar so I can walk you through it AND hear what happened on your end: calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview (https://calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview)

Honestly, option 3 is the one I'd value most. We're a small team, you'd be one of our first Calumet City customers, and feedback from someone who almost ordered is more useful to us than feedback from someone who already did. I'll make it worth your time.

Thanks for trying — and sorry it didn't work the first time.

Anthony Ivy
Founder, Uncle May's Produce
(312) 972-2595
anthony@unclemays.com

subject: Barbara — quick question from Uncle May's
body_plain:
Hi Barbara,

I'm Anthony, the founder of Uncle May's Produce. I was looking through our records this week and saw you ordered from us once but haven't been back. I'm not writing to push you to order again — I'm writing because I'd really like to know why.

We're a few weeks into the Chicago delivery side of the business, and we just relaunched as a build-your-own catalog (you can pick exactly what goes in your order, no fixed boxes). Before we put more money into ads or new products, I'd rather hear from the people who already tried us:

- What made you try us in the first place?
- What about the experience didn't quite work?
- What would have to be true for you to come back?

If you have 15 minutes in the next week or two, I'd be genuinely grateful for a quick phone call: https://calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview (https://calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview). I'll send you a $20 Uncle May's credit as a thank you for the time, no strings, you can use it or share it with someone.

You can also just reply to this email with a sentence or two if a call isn't possible. Either way, the honest answer is more useful to me than a polite one.

Thank you for trying us.

Anthony Ivy
Founder, Uncle May's Produce
(312) 972-2595
anthony@unclemays.com

subject: Brandy — was there an issue with your Uncle May's order?
body_plain:
Hi Brandy,

I'm Anthony, the founder of Uncle May's Produce. You tried to place an order with us 4 times and it never quite went through. I wanted to reach out personally rather than have you get an automated reminder. You were looking at the starter — I'm guessing it was either a payment hiccup, a browser issue, or you just got pulled away. We pushed several fixes to the checkout this week so it should behave better than it did then.

If you still want to give us a try, three ways forward — pick whichever is easiest:

1. Try checkout again at https://unclemays.com/shop (https://unclemays.com/shop) — code FRESH10 for $10 off your first order ($25 minimum).
2. Call/text me at (312) 972-2595 and I'll take the order in 5 minutes.
3. Grab 10 minutes on my calendar so I can walk you through it AND hear what happened on your end: https://calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview (https://calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview).

Honestly, option 3 is the one I'd value most. We're a small team a few weeks into testing this, and feedback from someone who almost ordered tells us more than feedback from someone who already did.

Thank you for trying us — and sorry it didn't work the first time.

Anthony Ivy
Founder, Uncle May's Produce
(312) 972-2595
anthony@unclemays.com

Honestly, option 3 is the one I'd value most. We're a small team a few weeks into testing this, and feedback from someone who almost ordered tells us more than feedback from someone who already did.

Thank you for trying us — and sorry it didn't work the first time.

Anthony Ivy
Founder, Uncle May's Produce

subject: Doina — quick thank-you, and a question
body_plain:
Hi Doina,

I'm Anthony at Uncle May's Produce. You're not a typical customer email I'd send — you've been on the subscription longer than almost anyone else and you're the reason that side of the business has any continuity at all. I wanted to reach out personally, just to say thank you for sticking with us through all of the changes this year.

Two reasons I'm writing:

1. Nothing is changing for you. Your weekly Wednesday delivery and the price you've been paying are staying exactly as they are. We've stopped signing up new subscriptions for now and pivoted to a build-your-own catalog for new customers, but your account is grandfathered and we're not touching it. If anything ever needs to change, I'll call you first.

2. I'd love 15 minutes of your time. I'm putting together materials for some early-stage investors and your perspective on what's worked, what hasn't, and what kept you ordering would be incredibly valuable. Anything you say can stay between us if you'd rather it not be quoted — even off-the-record context would help me a lot. If you're open to it, grab a time that works: https://calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview (https://calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview). Or just reply to this email and we can do it asynchronously.

Thank you again for being one of the very few customers who said yes early and meant it. I won't forget it.

Anthony Ivy
Founder, Uncle May's Produce
(312) 972-2595
anthony@unclemays.com

subject: A direct reply to your survey answer
body_plain:
Hi there,

I'm Anthony, the founder of Uncle May's Produce. You answered our Facebook survey a few weeks back with:

  "Goode foods, ramps, sweet corn"

I read every response personally and wanted to write back rather than send an automated thank-you.

Where we land on what you asked for:
- Good foods — that's the bet. Black-farmer-grown produce, pasture-raised protein, no consolidator markup. Chicago delivery on Wednesdays.
- Ramps — yes. They're in the catalog as of last week (we had to add them back after a customer specifically asked).
- Sweet corn — not yet. Our farmer partner (Run A Way Buckers Club, Pembroke IL) doesn't grow it. Onboarding a second supplier is a real consideration and your feedback is one of the data points pushing us toward it.

If you'd like to try a first order with the ramps in it, code FRESH10 is $10 off, $25 minimum cart:
unclemays.com/shop (https://unclemays.com/shop)

The conversation I'd actually rather have is the longer one. If you can spare 10–15 minutes for a phone call, I'd love to hear what's pulling you toward sweet corn specifically (we get asked for it a lot — there's a story there I want to understand): calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview (https://calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview). I'll send a $20 Uncle May's credit as thanks for the time.

Thanks for being one of the people who took the survey seriously.

Anthony Ivy
Founder, Uncle May's Produce
(312) 972-2595
anthony@unclemays.com

subject: Marian — about the chicken
body_plain:
Hi Marian,

I'm Anthony, the founder of Uncle May's Produce. You answered our quick "what would you want in your box" question on the website with one word — "Chicken." That's the most useful one-word answer I've gotten this month. I wanted to write back personally.

We carry whole pasture-raised chicken from our farmer partner Run A Way Buckers Club in Pembroke, IL. About 4–5 lb, $32, available every Wednesday delivery. Also farm-fresh eggs at $5.99/dozen if that helps.

If you want to give it a try, your first order is $10 off with code FRESH10, $25 minimum cart, Chicago + south-suburb delivery on Wednesdays:
unclemays.com/shop (https://unclemays.com/shop)

If you'd rather have a quick conversation — or you meant something specific by "chicken" that we don't currently carry (parts vs whole, organic certification, halal, etc.) — I'd love 10 minutes to hear it: calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview (https://calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview). I'll send a $20 Uncle May's credit as thanks for the time.

Either way — thank you for taking the survey seriously enough to answer.

Anthony Ivy
Founder, Uncle May's Produce
(312) 972-2595
anthony@unclemays.com

subject: Marian — thank you for the feedback. A direct reply.
body_plain:
Hi Marian,

I'm Anthony, the founder of Uncle May's Produce. You answered our quick survey on Facebook a few weeks back and told us:

  "A variety of seasonal vegetables. Greens, tomatoes, garlic, beans/peas, radish."
    "Drop off option, affordability and variety."

I'm not sending an automated thank-you. I read every response personally and I wanted to write back with what your answer actually changed for us — and where I have to be honest about what we can and can't do.

What we can do today, based on what you asked for:
- Greens (kale, salad mix, chard, romaine), garlic (when in season), beans (we have black, pinto, kidney as dry pantry beans plus fresh peas in season), radishes — all currently in the catalog. You can pick exactly what you want, no fixed boxes. $25 minimum.
- Drop-off (delivery) is our default — Wednesdays 5–8 PM CT, $7.99 flat across the South Side and south suburbs. Free pickup at Polsky in Hyde Park if you'd rather.
- Affordability — entry-priced items start at $1.50 (carrots) and $3.00 (Tuscan kale, romaine head). Use code FRESH10 for $10 off your first order.

What we honestly can't do right now:
- Tomatoes. Our farmer partner (Run A Way Buckers Club, in Pembroke IL) doesn't grow them. Putting them in the box would mean a second supplier, which is a real decision — your feedback is one of the data points pushing us to think about it. Not yet, but maybe soon.

If you'd like to give it a try, here's the link with the discount baked in:
unclemays.com/shop (https://unclemays.com/shop) (code FRESH10).

And honestly, the conversation I'd rather have is the longer one — 10–15 minutes on the phone or video, just to hear what would actually move the needle for you. If you can spare that, grab any time: calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview (https://calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview). I'll send you a $20 Uncle May's credit as a thank you for the time, no strings.

Thank you for being one of the people who took the survey seriously.

Anthony Ivy
Founder, Uncle May's Produce
(312) 972-2595
anthony@unclemays.com

subject: Linda — your Uncle May's order didn't go through, can I help?
body_plain:
Hi Linda,

I'm Anthony, the founder of Uncle May's Produce. You came through to checkout last Wednesday afternoon with a family box, black beans, and beef short ribs — delivery to N. Richmond — but the order never finished. I wanted to reach out personally rather than have you get an automated reminder.

A guess: it looked like you came in from a Google search on an iPad inside the Google app browser. We've seen Stripe's payment form misbehave inside in-app browsers — it's a known issue and not anything you did. If that was the case, I'm sorry — that's on us, not you.

If the box still sounds good, three ways forward:

1. Open this in regular Safari, not the Google app, and the payment form should behave: unclemays.com/shop (https://unclemays.com/shop) (use code FRESH10 for $10 off, $25 minimum).
2. Call/text me at (312) 972-2595 and I'll take the order in 5 minutes — no app browser nonsense.
3. Grab 10 minutes on my calendar — I'd walk you through the order AND love to hear what brought you to us: calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview (https://calendly.com/anthony-unclemays/uncle-may-s-quick-interview)

Honestly, option 3 is the one I'd appreciate most. We're a few weeks into the Chicago delivery side of the business and your perspective on what was attractive (and what got in the way) would help us a lot.

Thanks for trying us.

Anthony Ivy
Founder, Uncle May's Produce
(312) 972-2595
anthony@unclemays.com
